title: Reading a Venn diagram
problem: A Venn diagram shows events $A$ and $B$ within a sample space of $20$ equally likely outcomes. There are $6$ outcomes in $A$ only, $4$ outcomes in $B$ only, $3$ outcomes in both $A$ and $B$, and $7$ outcomes in neither. Find $P(A \text{ or } B)$.

steps.instruction: Identify the outcomes that make up $A$ or $B$
steps.working: $A$ or $B$ includes every outcome inside at least one of the two circles: $A$ only, $B$ only, and both $A$ and $B$.

title: Filling in a Venn diagram
problem: In a class of $30$ students, $18$ play sport, $14$ study music, and $7$ do both. A student is chosen at random. Find $P(\text{neither sport nor music})$.

steps.instruction: Find the number in each region

title: Using the addition rule to find P(A and B)
problem: For two events $A$ and $B$: $P(A) = \dfrac{3}{5}$, $P(B) = \dfrac{1}{2}$, and $P(A \text{ or } B) = \dfrac{4}{5}$. Find $P(A \text{ and } B)$.

steps.working: $$P(A \text{ or } B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \text{ and } B)$$

steps.instruction: Rearrange to make $P(A \text{ and } B)$ the subject
steps.working: $$P(A \text{ and } B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \text{ or } B)$$

steps.instruction: Convert to a common denominator and simplify
